published_prior_art.cite: LeCam1986 / GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026: indistinguishable or colliding observable laws imply no uniformly informative test.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: shared-completion LP ADE bounds, not a sparse exposure-map specification-testing frontier.

published_prior_art.cite: GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026 gives broad impossibility and a network-linear-in-means positive case; LeCam1986 gives generic TV testing logic, but neither states the sparse K-support collision quotient frontier.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: hidden-edge compatibility for ADE bounds, not a noncorner TV-separation witness for threshold exposure-map testing.

published_prior_art.cite: GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026 Section 4 positive case is no-interference vs network-linear-in-means, not finite sparse-threshold exposure fibers with unknown graph support.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: finite endpoint certificates for ADE bounds, not pointwise collision-triggered sparse random-set reporting.

published_prior_art.cite: BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011 gives general random-set sharp regions; SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 gives fixed-canonical exposure bounds, but neither links a pointwise sparse exposure collision to the reporting rule.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The collision quotient \mathcal Q_K is defined with edges only for unconstrained image intersections, but Conjecture 1 and §10 say the critical statistic is equivalently the minimum over LP-dual separating certificates indexed by those edges; \Lambda_K ranges over all null-alternative pairs, so non-edges or relevance-constrained slices can attain the minimum and are omitted.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine \mathcal Q_K as a full pair-indexed quotient with edge labels for intersection/separation status, or replace the edge-indexed certificate claim by a certificate family indexed over all null-alternative exposure-system pairs.
Add a §9 corollary showing that the revised quotient reduces to the ordinary Le Cam two-simple-hypothesis TV distance when |\mathcal E_0|=|\mathcal E_{1,K}|=1 and both fibers are singleton laws.
For Conjecture 3, state the support-function form of \mathsf C_K(P) explicitly so the gap relative to Beresteanu-Molchanov-Molinari is not just the generic convex-hull sharp-region construction.

recommended_next_step: Patch the \mathcal Q_K indexing/coherence issue, then rerun novelty review without changing the flagship sparse-collision kernel.
